architecture_domain.notes: A real shared Recording store, hierarchy, serializers, and generic events/comments/boosts/subscriptions support many resource types, but lifecycle propagation stops at immediate children and dictionary models weakly enforce parent/bucket invariants.
evidence: submission/server.py:230-276 and 382-428 centralize recordings and cross-cutting state in shared primitives.
submission/server.py:559-579 cascades status only to direct children; a live board archive left a grandchild comment active.

endpoint_coverage.notes: All 203 canonical operations are registered with exact methods/templates; 202 reach their intended handlers and one canonical GET is shadowed by an earlier generic route.
evidence: AST comparison found 203/203 OpenAPI operations registered across 131 paths, plus 80 extra routes.
The 203-operation HTTP sweep found one 500: GET /{accountId}/projects/recordings.json, shadowed by submission/server.py:2335-2340 before its intended handler at 2451-2476.

behavioral_depth.notes: Process-lifetime CRUD and cross-cutting behavior are substantial and demonstrably stateful, with gaps in recursive lifecycle invariants and shallow clone/template workflows.
evidence: Live project create/get/update/trash and todo create/complete/uncomplete/trash persisted and changed subsequent reads/lists.
Live message comment, boost, subscription, event, archive, and activate flows updated counts and status.
submission/server.py:2009-2014 shallow-clones only a tool root, and 4694-4729 reports completed template construction without materializing template content.

http_contract.notes: Core schemas, URLs, errors, creation/deletion statuses, and pagination are strong, but a shadowed route, success-status mismatches, permissive required bodies, and regex path matching create material contract defects.
evidence: Live pagination returned 15 items, X-Total-Count 21, a rel=next Link, and six items on page 2.
PUT /{accountId}/my/profile.json returned 200 although openapi.json:8620-8647 requires 204; question-answer update has the same 200-versus-204 defect at server.py:4469-4479.
A live malformed path /1/projects/1000Xjson matched project 1000 because server.py:2066-2071 does not escape route literals.

seed_fidelity.notes: The required owner, eight-person sample cast, project, six tools, and rich linked content are stable and navigable; current-time touches make some seeded updated_at values nondeterministic.
evidence: server.py --check reported 9 people, 1 project, 87 recordings, 5 messages, 10 todos, 13 cards, and 16 chat lines.
Live traversal found Launch the new website, all eight named sample people, dock IDs 2001-2006, 5 messages, 2 todo lists, docs/uploads, schedule entries, and paginated chat.
Two fresh runs gave recording 3001 identical fixed created_at but different current-time updated_at values due to server.py:283-284 and 466-540.

request_hardening.notes: Bearer auth, tenancy checks, permissions, malformed JSON, limits, and structured errors work, while identifier safety, media types, required bodies, route parsing, and reset security have serious gaps.
evidence: Live requests returned 401 for missing/bad auth, 404 for the wrong account, and 400 for malformed JSON.
An invalid project ID returned 500; text/plain JSON returned 422; missing bodies returned 200 for out-of-office, gauge, and question-answer update.
POST /admin/reset is unauthenticated and enabled by default at submission/server.py:2137-2152; a live call erased mutations and reseeded the store.

operability.notes: The dependency-free server has health/readiness, environment configuration, logging, request IDs, CORS, shutdown, reset, and check seams, but global volatile state and insecure defaults limit production shape.
evidence: python3 server.py --check completed successfully with route and seed counts.
submission/server.py:4816-4843 and 5042-5127 implement request IDs, CORS, threading, signal shutdown, CLI configuration, and smoke checking.
State is process-only; wildcard CORS, unauthenticated reset, and owner-token disclosure are defaults.

code_quality.notes: Clear sections and reusable helpers make the required single-file package understandable, but stringly typed global dictionaries, repetition, and concrete router/lifecycle bugs make extension risky.
evidence: Errors, store operations, serializers, pagination, routing, and transport are centralized in named sections.
The 5,131-line module mixes all layers and repeats many handler patterns; the first-match regex router caused a real canonical endpoint failure.

scope_honesty.notes: The submission claims production-shaped completeness, yet several partial or hollow operations return successful responses instead of explicit unsupported/partial failures.
evidence: submission/server.py:3-18 claims no hollow OpenAPI surface.
Question reminders always return [] at server.py:4027-4029; tool cloning is root-only at 2009-2014; template construction reports completed for an empty project at 4694-4729.
Missing required bodies still produced successful mutations for multiple canonical operations.

summary: A highly complete, process-stateful single-file prototype with full route registration, rich deterministic seed data, and broad working CRUD/cross-cutting behavior. It falls short of production-grade fidelity through one shadowed canonical endpoint, shallow lifecycle propagation, several HTTP/request-validation violations, insecure reset/credential defaults, and successful responses for partial behavior.
